prompt: Invent an original, vivid metaphor for the experience of learning a new language as an adult. Explain it in three sentences.
response: Learning a new language as an adult is like trying to build a delicate house of glass while wearing thick winter mittens—every movement is awkward, every placement uncertain, and the risk of shattering your fragile construction feels constant. Your hands (or in this case, your brain) are clumsy and unaccustomed to the precise movements required, fumbling with unfamiliar sounds and grammatical structures that seem to slip and slide just beyond your grasp. Yet with patience and persistent practice, those mittens gradually become thinner, your movements more deliberate, until eventually you're crafting intricate linguistic windows and doorways with a surprising new dexterity.
